Book a CourtNew to Pickleball?

Organized matchmaking
for all skill levels.

You’ll find there’s something for everyone on our events calendar. New to pickleball? Take a Quick Start class. Know how to play but need some experience? Try a Green Friendlies. Looking for leveled group play? Join our Blue Square social plays. Ready to leave it all on the court? Register for a league or competitive ladder. Not sure where to start? Contact us and let’s find the best fit for you!

Academy

Grow your game.

Customized, common sense coaching for beginners to 5.0 level players using our Four Bases framework.

Friendlies

Relax, it’s only pickleball.

Open to all levels, with a focus on lower level players. Meet new friends and develop your game.

Social Play

Play with others at your level.

Skill leveled gameplay with elements of fun, social, and no-icing-out etiquette.

Competitive Play

Leave it all on the court!

Play-to-win, including icing out stronger players, exploiting weaknesses, and overcoming tournament jitters.

Calendar

MIDDLETON
RYE

NEPC Skill Ratings

Our clubs have a diverse player base with varying levels of tournament experience, both regionally and nationally. This allows us to maintain accurate benchmarks for each skill level on par with established tournaments. For instance, our 3.5 male and female players are competitive with players rated between 3.5 and 3.9 in regional/national tournaments. Because we don’t differentiate senior players, some who excel in the 3.5 bracket at senior tournaments may find that they find their best play in our 3.0 skill bucket at NEPC events.

Play Video
Play Video

Etiquette and Sportsmanship

New England Pickleball Club strives to provide fair play for all - no matter what level you are.

Events FAQs

You’ve got questions, we’ve got answers. If our FAQs don’t address your question, just contact us and we’ll happily respond.

How does NEPC determine skill ratings?

NEPC reviews all available information to determine the most accurate skill rating for each player. This comprehensive approach includes evaluating performance in our competitive leagues, social play events, as well as any external tournaments. Our goal is to use a wide range of data to make the fairest skill rating decisions possible.

‍

If you’re interested in having your skill level assessed, please let any NEPC staff know. If you’re new to NEPC and unsure about your skill level, it’s better to self-rate conservatively (if you are between a 3.0 and 3.5, self-rate as a 3.0). All players new to the NEPC will have their self-rating reviewed and verified by staff during club play.

What if an event is full?

Always use the waitlist on CourtReserve if you can’t get into something. This is how we know to add more courts to the event. Please know that if you are on the waitlist you can be added to an event up to an hour prior, so if you are no longer available please remove yourself from the waitlist.

What if I have to cancel?

We keep our events with no or minimal subs, so cancellations can leave us short players, which is no fun for anyone. Canceling within 24 hours of your booking is subject to a fee of 50% of the total cost. No-shows or failing to cancel before your start time are subject to a fee of 100% of the total cost.

‍

Guests added to private reservations will be billed to the reserving player unless adjustments are made at the front desk. Court shoes are required for all bookings, and court assignments may change prior to check-in to maximize utilization and staffing.

Can non-members participate in events?

Yes! Non-members can sign up 4 days in advance for private courts in Middleton and 3 days in advance in Rye or for any group play or 30 days in advance for classes.

Still have a questions?
We're here to help you!
